title: eriogonum thymoides subsp. typicum
intro: Eriogonum thymoides subsp. typicum, also known as Bigelow's buckwheat or California buckwheat, is a plant species in the buckwheat family (Polygonaceae) that is native to California and Oregon. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic, antibacterial, and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to soothe wounds, reduce pain, and combat infections. The health benefits of Eriogonum thymoides subsp. typicum include reducing inflammation, fighting off infections, and promoting wound healing. This plant can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or salve, and is often used in herbal medicine to treat various ailments, including wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues.
